sourceMovie.title: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It (2021) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Michael Chaves's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Body Cam
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Body Cam (2020) captures the horror appeal of The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It without simply imitating it. The mystery streak running through both films is what puts Body Cam at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror appeal of The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into The Conjuring (2013). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Scream 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Scream 2 (1997) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Insidious: Chapter 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Insidious: Chapter 2 (2013) is the kind of film The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Insidious: Chapter 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Insidious: Chapter 3 shares The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It's gift for horror — the 2015 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Pope's Exorcist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It compelling — the horror, the thriller — shows up in The Pope's Exorcist (2023) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Countdown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The horror energy of The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It is alive in Countdown (2019), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Deliver Us from Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Deliver Us from Evil (2014) operates in the same horror as The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Panic Room
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Panic Room (2002) belongs to the same conversation as The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It — both anchor their thriller in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It's horror stayed with you, The Conjuring: Last Rites (2025) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The dark through-line is the real common thread between them.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It?
faq.answer: Try Body Cam, The Conjuring, Scream 2—each shares DNA with The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It?
faq.answer: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It (2021) is best described as Horror, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Conjuring Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
